takeaways: Senior housing M&A accelerates — Sonida and CNL Healthcare complete a $1.8B merger creating a $3B enterprise, while Pegasus surpasses 50 communities by absorbing 10 former Brookdale properties.

Capital is flooding back into CRE — CLO issuance hit $11.2B in early 2026 (+34% YoY) and transaction activity reached a 4-year high in February, but rising 10-year Treasury yields (+34 bps in 2 weeks) are pressuring fixed-rate deal economics.

AI goes mission-critical in real estate — Zillow launched AI Mode today, proptech VC hit $1.7B in January alone (+176% YoY), and AI-driven platforms are cutting multifamily operating costs 10–15% as adoption jumps from 5% to 92% of CRE companies in three years.

items.headline: Sonida and CNL Healthcare Complete $1.8B Merger, Creating $3B Senior Housing Giant
items.summary: Sonida Senior Living finalized its acquisition of CNL Healthcare Properties in a cash-and-stock deal valued at ~$1.8B, producing a combined enterprise worth $3B. The merger significantly expands Sonida's operational scale and positions it as a dominant owner-operator in a consolidating market. For Lloyd Jones and other regional operators, this signals continued M&A pressure and the growing importance of operational differentiation.

items.headline: CRE CLO Issuance Surges 34% YoY, Nearing Record $45B Annual Pace
items.summary: CRE CLO issuance reached $11.2B in early 2026 — a 34% year-over-year increase — putting full-year projections near the 2021 record of $45B. Multifamily assets dominate at ~70% of collateral. This surge signals renewed institutional appetite for CRE debt, which could benefit senior housing operators seeking recapitalization or refinancing options.

items.headline: Zillow Launches AI Mode — A Conversational, Guided Home Search Experience
items.summary: Zillow today debuted 'AI Mode,' a conversational interface that guides buyers and renters through listings, affordability analysis, and agent connections. The launch marks a pivotal shift in consumer real estate UX and reflects the broader industry trend of AI becoming the primary interface layer. Senior housing operators should expect similar AI-driven search tools to reshape how prospective residents discover communities.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Report Elevated Stress; 40% Considering Leaving Their Roles
items.summary: New DDI research reveals that 71% of executives and managers are experiencing heightened stress, with 40% actively considering leaving their positions. Manager engagement dropped from 30% to 27% in 2025 — a rare and concerning decline. For senior housing operators managing large front-line workforces, this data underscores the urgency of investing in middle-management support, recognition, and development.

items.headline: Proptech VC Investment Hit $1.7B in January 2026 Alone — Up 176% YoY
items.summary: After a record $16.7B in proptech venture investment in 2025 (+68% YoY), momentum accelerated further with $1.7B deployed in January 2026 — a 176% jump vs. January 2025. Three new proptech unicorns have emerged since July, all AI-powered. AI-centered proptech companies are growing at 42% annually vs. 24% for non-AI peers, reshaping competitive dynamics across property management and leasing.

items.headline: Pegasus Senior Living Surpasses 50 Communities with 10-Property Texas Expansion
items.summary: Pegasus Senior Living crossed the 50-community threshold by adding 10 former Brookdale properties in Texas via a new management agreement effective April 1. The move exemplifies the ongoing operator consolidation trend, where mid-tier operators are scaling rapidly by absorbing assets from larger players shedding non-core portfolios. Lloyd Jones should monitor similar management agreement opportunities as portfolios continue to restructure.

items.headline: Commercial Mortgage Rates Sit at 5.39% as 10-Year Treasury Spikes 34 bps
items.summary: Commercial mortgage rates as of March 25 range from 4.99% to 8.75%, with a low-end benchmark of 5.39%. The Fed has held its target rate at 3.50–3.75% since January, but a 34-basis-point surge in the 10-year Treasury over the past two weeks is pushing fixed-rate financing costs higher. Operators and investors should factor in rate volatility when modeling near-term acquisition or refinancing timelines.

items.headline: CEO AI Accountability Doubles: 75% Now Lead AI Strategy, 50% Say Jobs Depend on It
items.summary: CEO ownership of AI strategy doubled year-over-year: 75% of CEOs now serve as their organization's primary AI decision-maker, and half believe their job security hinges on whether AI investments deliver returns. Companies plan to double AI spending in 2026. For senior housing executives, this signals that AI ROI is no longer a back-office concern — it is a board-level and CEO-level accountability issue.

items.headline: CRE Transaction Volume Expected to Rise 15–20% in 2026 as Capital Returns
items.summary: Colliers forecasts a 15–20% increase in CRE sales volume in 2026, backed by stabilizing pricing, improving leasing fundamentals, and re-entering institutional and cross-border capital. CRE activity hit a 4-year high in February, rising 7% month-over-month per LightBox data. Senior housing assets, with strong occupancy recovery trends, are well-positioned to capture a share of this renewed capital interest.

items.headline: EliseAI Automates 24/7 Multifamily Operations — From Tours to Lease Audits
items.summary: EliseAI's agentic platform is handling correspondence, tour scheduling, lease audits, and maintenance coordination around the clock for multifamily landlords. The share of CRE companies running AI pilots jumped from 5% to 92% in just three years. AI-driven property management platforms are demonstrating 10–15% operating cost reductions — a model with direct applicability to senior housing communities managing high-volume resident services.

items.headline: Erickson Senior Living Breaks Ground on 1,000-Unit Maryland Campus
items.summary: Erickson Senior Living has commenced construction on Oxford Hills in Clarksville, Maryland — a 62-acre independent living campus with 1,000 units, dining venues, fitness facilities, and outdoor amenities. First residents are expected in late 2028. The project reflects sustained confidence in large-format independent living developments in high-demand mid-Atlantic markets.

items.headline: AI Wellness Coaching Goes Mainstream: Personalized, Biomarker-Driven Health in 2026
items.summary: 2026 is being called the inflection year for personalized wellness, driven by AI coaches, at-home biomarker testing, and smart wearables that adapt health recommendations in real time. The Global Wellness Summit reports 95% of workers now see physical, mental, emotional, and social wellbeing as interconnected. Senior housing operators offering resident wellness programs have an opportunity to differentiate through data-driven, personalized approaches.

items.headline: Block Inc. Cuts 4,000 Jobs as 2026 Layoff Wave Tops 1,600 Companies
items.summary: Block Inc. (Jack Dorsey) shed 4,000 positions on March 20, while CBS News cut 6% of its workforce on March 21. Since January 1, 2026, more than 1,621 companies have announced mass layoffs. While concentrated in tech and media, the pattern reflects ongoing labor market restructuring that may influence consumer sentiment, discretionary spending, and demand for cost-efficient senior living options.

items.headline: Illinois Developer Proposes $50M Mixed-Use Project with 167-Unit Senior Living Component
items.summary: Leo Brown Group is seeking approval for Traditions of St. Charles in Illinois — a ~$50M development combining 306 market-rate apartments with 69 independent living, 64 assisted living, and 34 memory care units. The mixed-use format reflects a growing model for integrating senior housing into broader residential communities, potentially improving absorption and reducing stigma.

items.headline: Integrated Corporate Wellness Programs Deliver Up to 20% Productivity Gains
items.summary: Companies tying wellness programs directly to leadership KPIs and business outcomes are reporting up to 20% higher productivity, according to WebMD Health Services. CEOs are giving HR mandates and budgets aligned with retention and performance metrics. For senior housing operators with high caregiver turnover, targeted wellness investments may offer measurable ROI in both retention and service quality.

items.headline: CREFC CEO Lisa Pendergast to Retire in August After a Decade Leading CRE Finance
items.summary: The CRE Finance Council announced that President and CEO Lisa Pendergast will retire on August 3, 2026, ending a decade-long tenure. CREFC simultaneously held a March 25 webinar on new banking regulatory capital proposals, with a 90-day comment period underway. The regulatory proposals could affect CRE lending standards and credit availability — worth monitoring for acquisition financing strategy.

items.headline: Movement Snacks Replace Traditional Workouts as Sedentary Culture Antidote
items.summary: Short bursts of movement, stretching, and mobility exercises scattered throughout the workday are gaining traction as a practical solution to sedentary work culture. Active Wellness identifies this as one of 20 top trends shaping how people move and feel in 2026. For senior housing communities, incorporating micro-movement programming into daily resident schedules aligns with this broader behavioral shift.
